operating_state: Total revenue increased 5% to $10.6 billion in 2025 driven by property segment growth and services expansion. International segments delivered broad-based growth with double-digit increases in Europe and Africa & APAC, offset by Latin America declining due to foreign exchange headwinds and increased revenue reserves. The company added approximately 2,230 communications sites globally and maintains over $54 billion in non-cancellable tenant lease revenue with tenant churn at approximately 2%.
cost_structure: Property segment gross margin increased 4% with margin expansion in most regions except Latin America. Operating expenses increased modestly with personnel costs and canceled construction expenses partially offset by decreased stock-based compensation. Operating lease costs increased while variable lease costs decreased, with total depreciation expense remaining stable.
material_events: Completed $3.0 billion senior notes issuance with maturities from 2030-2035 while repaying $3.2 billion of maturing debt. Sold South Africa Fiber business generating $138 million proceeds and $54 million gain. Repurchased $365 million of common stock and paid $3.1 billion in dividends. AT&T Mexico dispute resulted in partial payment resumption with remaining amounts in escrow pending August 2026 arbitration.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation losses totaled $809 million in 2025 compared to $308 million gains in 2024, primarily from intercompany note exposures and subsidiary functional currency fluctuations. The company designated approximately €4.7 billion of EUR-denominated senior notes as net investment hedges. Revenue reserves increased in Brazil and Mexico reflecting regional credit deterioration.
forward_outlook: 2026 capital expenditures projected at $1.8-1.9 billion including approximately 1,700-2,300 new communications sites globally and data center assets. Management maintains purchase options for approximately $5.0 billion of Verizon towers with tranches expiring 2034-2047. Data center business positioned for AI workload growth through hybrid-cloud deployments with purpose-built capacity for higher-density requirements.
uncertainty_profile: AT&T Mexico arbitration outcome remains uncertain with potential material negative impact representing $300 million annual tenant revenue exposure. Customer concentration risk persists with 59% of revenues from four telecommunications customers creating sensitivity to creditworthiness changes. Asset retirement obligations increased to $2.5 billion with estimated undiscounted future cash outlay of $4.6 billion including timing and cost estimation uncertainties.
